data.title: Orsted Expects Earnings to Rise This Year as It Works to Shore Up Balance Sheet
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Orsted guided for slightly higher earnings this year as some wind and solar farms ramp up and it expects to receive higher payments from wind-farm contracts to account for inflation.

The Danish renewable-energy company expects earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization excluding new partnerships and cancellation fees at between 25 billion and 28 billion kroner ($3.49 billion-$3.9 billion), up from 24.75 billion kroner in 2024.

The company announced late Wednesday that it will cut its investment program by 25% in the period to 2030 to ensure a stronger balance sheet and support a solid investment grade rating.

The news comes days after the company parted ways with Chief Executive Officer Mads Nipper, who has been replaced by Rasmus Errboe, its former deputy chief executive and chief commercial officer.

On Thursday, Orsted reported fourth-quarter Ebitda excluding new partnerships and cancellation fees--the company's preferred metric--of 7.55 billion kroner, down from 8.62 billion kroner a year prior.

The net loss widened to 6.08 billion kroner from a loss of 284 million kroner, as revenue fell 2.1% to 21.08 billion kroner.

A FactSet consensus had seen net profit at 2.74 billion kroner on revenue of 26.02 billion kroner.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: Nissan and Honda End Merger Talks Over Restructuring Dispute
data.description:
Nissan Motor Co has ended merger talks with Honda Motor , halting plans for a deal to create the world's third-largest auto group, Kyodo News reported Thursday.

The decision follows Honda's proposal for Nissan to become a subsidiary, which faced strong opposition within Nissan.

Honda, frustrated with Nissan's slow turnaround, had pushed for the change despite the original plan to merge under a holding company by 2026.

Mitsubishi Motors, Nissan's alliance partner, is also likely to avoid the talks due to concerns over reduced autonomy.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: LGI Homes Closes 219 Homes in January
data.description:
LGI Homes reported late Wednesday home closings of 219 units in January.

The homebuilder had 148 active selling communities as of Jan. 31, it said.

($1 = 0.8017 pounds)

data.title: Czechia Industrial Output Continues to Drop
data.description:
Industrial production in the Czech Republic decreased by 3% year-on-year in December 2024, following a 2.7% fall in the previous month.

This marked the third consecutive month of contraction and the sharpest drop since June.

Output for mining and quarrying declined sharply (-14.7% vs 1.9% in November), while that for manufacturing dropped at the same pace as in the prior month (at -3.1%).

At the same time, production growth slowed for electricity, gas, steam, and air-conditioning supply (0.2% vs 0.7%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output fell by 1.6% in December, after a 1.8% decrease in November.

In 2024, industrial activity dropped by 1.4%.

data.title: KFC India operator Sapphire beats profit estimates as cheaper menu drives demand
data.description:
Sapphire Foods India , which operates Pizza Hut and KFC restaurants in the country, reported a better-than-expected quarterly profit on Thursday, as its cheaper food items helped boost demand from budget-conscious consumers.

The restaurant operator posted a profit of 119.8 million rupees ($1.37 million) for the third quarter ended December 31, up from a profit of 101.4 million rupees a year earlier.

Analysts, on average, had expected a profit of 116.8 million rupees, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 87.5750 Indian rupees)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
S&P 500 Turns Higher; Google, AMD in Focus

U.S. stocks closed with gains as earnings and economic data retook center stage in markets and political turbulence took a step back.

German Factory Orders Get Late-2024 Boost as Tariffs Loom

Manufacturing orders in Germany rose in December on aerospace demand, although any tentative signs of a recovery for the struggling industrial sector face the imminent threat of tariffs from the U.S.

Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes

In a speech to business leaders, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October.

BOE Expected to Cut Interest Rates. Why That's Good for the Dollar.

Investors are pricing in a 97% chance that the U.K. central bank lowers its key rate to 4.5% from 4.75% on Thursday.

Trump and Musk's Actions Amount to Significant Pullback on Financial Regulation

Agencies overseeing consumer financial affairs, wealth managers, and commodity traders all seem to be scaling back oversight activity.

Fannie, Freddie Privatization, Cost-Cutting and New Name Are Priorities for HUD Chief

Scott Turner says he plans to quickly launch a review to root out inefficiencies at the agency.

Why Trump Is Closing a Trade Exemption for China

Here's how to understand de minimis and how it turbocharged bargain platforms Shein and Temu.

How DeepSeek could deliver another blow to the stock market

DeepSeek's full impact isn't yet reflected in U.S. stock prices, says Don Townswick at Conning

Dining Out Now Comes With a 50 Cent 'Egg Surcharge'

Waffle House and other restaurants are responding to a bird flu-related egg shortage with an added fee.

Fed's Barkin Says Economy Is Fine. Government Policy Is a Wild Card.

It's too early to determine the impact of tariffs and other Trump policies on the economy, but the uncertainty is having an effect, the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond president said.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
AstraZeneca Expects More Sales, Earnings Growth After Results Beat Market Views

The pharmaceuticals major was boosted by high demand for its key drugs that offset a slight sales drop in China.

Eli Lilly's Results Already Disappointed the Street. More Details Come Thursday.

The company disclosed preliminary results in mid January, but it left out important metrics such as earnings per share for the fourth quarter.

Tesla sales are diving in Germany and across Europe too

Tesla sales careened lower in Germany in January, as sales of the electric vehicle company headed by Elon Musk tumble across Europe.

OpenAI Set to Make Super Bowl Ad Debut

The TV spot marks the artificial-intelligence company's biggest foray into consumer advertising to date.

Allstate Expects California Wildfires to Cost $1.1 Billion

In the fourth quarter, catastrophe losses took $315 million out of Allstate's homeowners-insurance business, up from $21 million the year before.

Amazon Earnings Are Today. Why It's All About AWS.

Wall Street expects Amazon Web Servicesrevenue of $28.8 billion for the quarter, an increase from last year's $24.2 billion.

Carl's Jr. Returns to Racy Advertising Years After It Abandoned 'Bikinis and Burgers'

The burger chain dropped its sexualized campaigns in 2017. Now the company has returned to that approach with an "unapologetic, bold" social-media personality.

Sonos to Cut 12% of Jobs in Further Restructuring Push

The audio-products maker expects to book restructuring charges of $15 million to $18 million as it reorganizes.

Hillenbrand Sells Majority Stake in Milacron to Bain Capital Affiliate

The provider of highly-engineered processing equipment and solutions will sell a 51% stake in the injection molding and extrusion business for $287 million.

Salesforce's Longtime Operating Chief Millham to Retire; Washington Named Successor and Financial Chief

Operating Chief Brian Millham to exit after more than 25 at the company. Incoming CFO Robin Washington has served on Salesforce's board since 2013.

data.title: Mediobanca suitor MPS bets on fees to stem profit drop
data.description:
State-backed Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) on Thursday posted a smaller than expected 5% quarterly drop in net income due to higher loan loss provisions and lower trading income.

MPS last month shocked Italy's financial world by launching a hostile all-share bid for bigger rival Mediobanca , seven-and-a-half years after a state bailout that drew a line under a decade-long crisis.

Net profit was 385 million euros ($400 million), sharply down from a year earlier when it had been boosted by a release of funds put aside for legal risks worth nearly 470 million euros.

Favourable court rulings have been a boon for MPS, which has been dogged by lawsuits after years of mismanagement.

Net fees, which have become the focus for banks as they try to counter the negative impact of lower rates on their lending business, grew by 5% quarter-on-quarter, more than offsetting the drop in net interest margin.

In the full year, net profit totalled 1.95 billion euros, beating a 1.82 billion euro forecast in an analyst consensus provided by the bank.

The annual result benefits from 506 million euros in tax credits linked to past losses, which MPS is able to use now that it has returned to churning profits.

MPS CEO Luigi Lovaglio has bet on 1.2 billion euros in tax credits that MPS could release if it has secured at least 50% plus one Mediobanca shares to convince investors in the rival to tender their shares in a deal where the chance for cost cuts is small.

Mediobanca has no branch network and makes money from consumer credit, where it partners with MPS, an investment in insurer Generali , and its wealth management and corporate investment banking businesses.

Touting the benefits of a tie-up, MPS said it had been able to grow its consumer loans by 21% in 2024, and wealth management inflows by 40%.

MPS' bid for Mediobanca came after the latest stake sale by the Treasury - which now owns only 11.7% of MPS - brought onboard as MPS shareholders two leading Mediobanca investors.

($1 = 0.9626 euros)

data.title: Anglo Platinum profit set to almost halve on weak prices, restructuring costs
data.description:
Anglo American Platinum expects headline earnings for the 2024 financial year to drop by as much as 46% on the back of persistently low metals prices and costs associated with restructuring and a demerger from its parent company.

The world's top producer of platinum group metals used to curb emissions in motor vehicles said it expects headline earnings between 7.6 billion rand and 9 billion rand ($408.8 million-$484.1 million) in the year ended December 31, down from 14 billion rand in 2023.

Amplats said dollar prices of palladium and rhodium fell by 24% and 30%, respectively, in the past year.

The platinum miner is set to be separated from the Anglo American group by mid-year, its CEO Craig Miller told Reuters on Wednesday.

The spinoff is part of the group's restructuring strategy, aimed at fighting off a $49 billion takeover bid from bigger rival BHP to focus on more profitable copper and iron ore assets.

Amplats said it had incurred 3.5 billion rand in costs associated with operational and corporate restructurings, including the demerger.

($1 = 18.5896 rand)

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Closes 2.6% Higher at 5059.38
Hang Seng Index Closes 1.4% Higher at 20891.62
Zhejiang Zhongxin Environmental Protection Technology Group
data.description:
Zhejiang Zhongxin Environmental Protection Technology Group started production of its first workshop at a factory in Thailand's Golden Pond Industrial Park after completing trial production.

The trial confirmed the advanced performance of the company's independently developed production equipment, surpassing current domestic standards, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The factory is expected to produce more than 30,000 tons of pulp-molded tableware in 2025, with ramp-up to full capacity of 50,000 tons by 2026.

Shares of the pulp tableware producer closed 1% higher Thursday.

data.title: OUE Healthcare Joint Venture Enters Strategic Partnership with Zhongda Hospital Southeast University
data.description:
OUE Healthcare , through its joint venture hospital, Changshu China Merchants - Lippo Obstetrics & Gynaecology Hospital, entered into a strategic partnership with Zhongda Hospital Southeast University, according to a company release on Wednesday.

Under the partnership, the two entities will establish a two-way 'green channel' for patient access to develop advanced clinica services.

The partnership will also focus on research, education, nursing and management.

Shares of the healthcare group were up nearly 4% in recent trading.

data.title: Volvo Car Shares Fall 6.2% After 4Q Earnings
Telenor Shares Rise 0.6% After 4Q Earnings
Sterling Falls Ahead of BOE Rate Decision — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling falls as investors exercise caution ahead of the Bank of England's interest-rate decision at 1200 GMT. The market is pricing in a 94% chance of the BOE cutting rates by 25 basis points, according to LSEG. This marks the first decision since the U.K. government bond selloff last month. "Indeed, at the height of the pound's slump, there had been speculation about whether the BOE would cut at all at this meeting," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. However, the latest inflation data were lower than expected and this supported market bets for another cut. Deutsche Bank expects a 8-1 vote in favor of a cut. GBP/USD falls 0.2% to 1.2476. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8325. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)
